body:
Fixes #6.

## Summary
- Start the static web demo in interactive demo mode by default so the first screen immediately shows an automated chat exchange.
- Add a live `Next dialog in Ns` status that updates once per second between randomized demo cycles.
- Add a default-off `Diagnostics` toggle that gates trace details, worker status, intent chips, evidence chips, and thinking steps.
- Preserve the requested case-study data, online research, issue screenshot, requirement extraction, and solution notes under `docs/case-studies/issue-6`.
- Update requirements, changelog, E2E coverage, and sync `Cargo.lock` with the existing `0.15.0` package version.

## Reproduction and Root Cause
Before this PR, the demo opened in manual mode, displayed diagnostic trace/intent/evidence details by default, and stored the next-dialog wait as a static string that did not change while the user waited. The new Playwright regression test first failed because the initial toggle rendered as `Demo` with `aria-pressed="false"`.

The root cause was demo-shell presentation state in `docs/demo`; the Rust engine, worker classifier, generated WebAssembly file, and datasets did not need behavior changes. Full raw evidence and analysis are in `docs/case-studies/issue-6/README.md`.

## Demo
![formal-ai issue 6 demo UI](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/issue-6-d8cf53772b56/docs/screenshots/issue-6-demo-ui.png?raw=true)

## Verification
- `node --check docs/demo/app.js`
- `cd tests/e2e && npm run test:local` (16 passed)
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo test --all-features --verbose`
- `cargo test --doc --verbose`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `rust-script scripts/check-changelog-fragment.rs`
- Playwright MCP visual verification on desktop and mobile viewports; screenshot committed above.

comments.body: Also I don't see any use of preview button (that is near send, so it should be removed). Also in the message for unknown prompt, we should generate prefilled GitHub issue link, so user can give feedback containing the history of the dialog, and also metadata like version and so on. Do it like we do in http://github.com/link-assistant/calculator and https://github.com/link-assistant/meta-expression There should be also way to report an issue on any dialog, without triggering the unknown intent. And also there should be some standard message on asking question of `Who are you?`. We also should support variations of that question.
